rationale_for_baseline_tier: A bilateral symmetric proximal PMR flare with intact distal neurology and no GCA features needs urgent next-day GP review to start low-dose steroids, with interim analgesia and warmth — a T2 baseline. The mandatory escalations are for GCA (sight-threatening) and cord compression, both emergencies.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Distal leg/arm weakness, numbness with a sensory level, or loss of bladder/bowel control
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests cord compression/myelopathy needing urgent imaging.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your arms or legs become weak, you get numbness with a band across your body, or you lose bladder/bowel control, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New temporal headache, scalp tenderness, jaw ache when chewing, or ANY visual change
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals giant cell arteritis — a sight-threatening emergency needing urgent high-dose steroids.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a headache over your temple, tender scalp, jaw ache when chewing, or any change in your vision, go to the emergency department immediately — this can threaten your sight.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult with PMR (GCA overlap risk)
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise the GCA safety-net; do not delay assessment for cranial/visual symptoms.
Ensure she can manage tonight; arrange help if needed.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a flare of polymyalgia rheumatica — inflammation of the big shoulder and hip muscles, not your nerves or bones. Keep warm, use heat and gentle movement, take an anti-inflammatory with food or paracetamol, and see your GP first thing tomorrow to start a low-dose steroid. Go to ED immediately if you get a temple headache, tender scalp, jaw ache when chewing, or any vision change, or if your arms/legs become weak or you lose bladder/bowel control.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent next-day GP; ED/000 for GCA or myelopathy features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED overnight if GCA or myelopathy red flags develop; otherwise see the GP in the morning.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a PMR flare; myelopathy and GCA screened negative on history; interim analgesia/warmth advised; urgent next-day GP review to commence low-dose steroids recommended; firm GCA safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without a GCA/myelopathy screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An emerging giant cell arteritis is missed and the patient loses vision, or a cord compression progresses to permanent deficit.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is too slow; urgent next-day GP steroid review (T2) with a GCA safety-net is preferred.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for a symmetric proximal flare with intact neurology is over-triage (unless GCA/myelopathy features).

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine neurology/temporal arteries or obtain ESR/CRP remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Screen myelopathy/GCA by history, reassure a PMR flare with interim analgesia, arrange urgent GP steroid review, and give a firm GCA safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either over-triage to an ambulance or reassure without a GCA safety-net.
